reasoning: Niger is experiencing extreme security challenges following the July 2023 coup, the subsequent expulsion of US and French military forces, and the influx of Russian private military contractors (Wagner/Africa Corps). Its neighbors in the Alliance of Sahel States (Mali and Burkina Faso) both hold Level 4 (Do Not Travel) advisories due to identical issues: widespread terrorism (JNIM, ISGS), high kidnapping risks, and limited US embassy capability. The US State Department had already upgraded Niger to Level 4 immediately following the 2023 coup. Even if it was temporarily lowered to Level 3, the ongoing departure of Western security guarantees and the high threat of terrorism mean Niger functions practically as a Level 4 environment. Given the May 2025 knowledge cutoff, it is highly likely that Niger has already received or will receive a Level 4 advisory by July 2025.
